title: Reconciling the Accuracy-Diversity Trade-off in Recommendations
authors: Kenny Peng, Manish Raghavan, Emma Pierson, Jon Kleinberg, and Nikhil Garg
source_version: WWW '24: Proceedings of the ACM Web Conference 2024, pp. 1318--1329; arXiv:2307.15142v1
main_caveat: Finite optimization, Bernoulli, uniform, bounded density/tail-mass, exponential, Pareto, Example 1 exact harmonic calibration, Corollary 1 concrete model existence, Theorems 1-3, Proposition 2's corrected finite/asymptotic homogeneity route, Lemma D.1/D.5 downstream routes, and Proposition 4 averaging/kernel checkpoint layers are exposed. The only remaining literal source gap is Proposition 4's continuous sphere/Laplace analytic certificate. Proposition 2's printed sharp finite constant and Lemma D.1(i)'s printed sign convention are documented as nonblocking source deviations.
human_summary: Proposition 2's printed finite bound appears to miss a factor of 2; Lean proves the corrected finite bound, which is sufficient for the asymptotic 1/2-homogeneity result. Fully formalizing the remaining result, Proposition 4, requires a general Laplace-principle-related analysis library.

human_summary_review.note: Preserve this human-written public summary unless a human explicitly asks to edit it.
